name: windows-triage-mcp
instructions: Use this backend for offline Windows baseline checks. UNKNOWN means not present in the local baseline and is neutral unless case evidence adds suspicion.

tools.name: wintriage_check_artifact
tools.description: Validate one Windows artifact (file/hash/filename/LOLBin/DLL) against local offline baselines.
tools.when_to_use: Use after identifying a Windows file, hash, filename, LOLBin, driver, or DLL from case evidence.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid escalating UNKNOWN results without additional suspicious evidence.
tools.output_notes: Baseline verdicts help prioritize; they do not prove maliciousness by themselves.

tools.name: wintriage_check_process_tree
tools.description: Validate a process parent-child relationship against the Windows process tree baseline.
tools.when_to_use: Use when process creation evidence shows a parent/child pair that needs baseline context.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid treating unusual parentage as malicious without corroborating execution or user context.
tools.output_notes: Returns expected/suspicious baseline context for the relationship.

tools.name: wintriage_check_system
tools.description: Validate Windows persistence/system configuration (service/scheduled_task/autorun) against OS-version baselines.
tools.when_to_use: Use for services, scheduled tasks, autoruns, and similar Windows persistence artifacts.
tools.output_notes: Use results to guide deeper inspection of suspicious persistence locations.

tools.name: wintriage_check_registry
tools.description: Check a registry key or value against the full registry baseline (requires the optional 12GB known_good_registry.db).
tools.when_to_use: Use when registry evidence needs comparison against a known-good Windows baseline.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid this if the optional registry database is unavailable; use narrower checks instead.
tools.output_notes: Registry baseline coverage depends on the optional local database.

tools.name: wintriage_check_pipe
tools.description: Check a named pipe against known Windows pipes and known C2 framework pipes.
tools.when_to_use: Use when named pipe evidence appears in process, EDR, Sysmon, or memory artifacts.
tools.output_notes: Known C2 pipe matches are investigation pivots requiring case corroboration.

tools.name: wintriage_server_status
tools.description: Report Windows triage backend readiness (health, db_stats, or all).
tools.when_to_use: Use as the backend health/readiness check before relying on baseline checks.
tools.output_notes: Health/status output only; it is not case evidence.
